question_text: A spring has a spring constant of 50 N/m. When a force of 10 N is applied to the spring, how much does it extend? Use the equation F = ke.
explanation: Using F = ke, we rearrange to find e = F/k. So e = 10 N ÷ 50 N/m = 0.2 m (or 20 cm). The extension tells us how much longer the spring becomes when the force is applied.
